reasoning_base: Core disease-modifying therapy for HFrEF, but this patient context is {context}.
guidance.current_med_absent: No clear current ARNI/ACEi/ARB therapy detected in the medication list.

conditional_reasoning.text: Low systolic BP increases risk of symptomatic hypotension during initiation or titration.
Abnormal potassium increases risk when RAAS-inhibiting therapy is intensified.

actions: Review whether the patient is already on ACEi/ARB/ARNI and avoid duplicate RAAS blockade.
If clinically stable, consider low-dose initiation or cautious titration rather than escalation at full dose.
guidance.avoid_prepend_action: Defer this class until the blocking safety issue is corrected or specialist review is completed.
guidance.caution_prepend_action: Treat this as a cautious/conditional option, not an automatic approval.

reasoning_base: Evidence-based beta blocker is core HFrEF therapy; patient context is {context}.
guidance.current_med_absent: No evidence-based beta blocker detected in the medication list.

conditional_reasoning.text: Low heart rate makes dose escalation unsafe without assessing symptoms, ECG, and conduction disease.
Low BP may limit titration, especially if dizziness, shock, or congestion is present.

actions: Continue if tolerated and clinically stable; avoid up-titration while HR/BP are limiting.
Check for decompensated HF, bradycardia symptoms, and AV block before any dose increase.
monitoring: Signs of congestion or acute decompensation

reasoning_base: MRA can reduce HFrEF morbidity/mortality, but renal function and potassium drive safety; patient context is {context}.
guidance.current_med_absent: No current MRA detected in the medication list.

conditional_reasoning.text: Reduced eGFR increases hyperkalemia and renal adverse-event risk.
Elevated potassium is a direct safety concern for MRA continuation or titration.

actions: Do not increase MRA dose when potassium is elevated or eGFR is severely reduced.
Consider holding or reducing MRA if hyperkalemia is confirmed; reassess after correction.
monitoring: Potassium and creatinine/eGFR promptly and after any change
Dietary potassium, supplements, NSAIDs, and RAAS combination risk

reasoning_base: SGLT2 inhibitor is a core HFrEF GDMT class and is often useful with CKD/diabetes; patient context is {context}.
guidance.current_med_absent: No current SGLT2 inhibitor detected in the medication list.

conditional_reasoning.text: Low eGFR requires product-specific threshold review before initiation.
Volume status should be reviewed because diuretic effect can worsen hypotension or dehydration.

actions: Consider initiation if no contraindication and eGFR meets product/guideline threshold.
Review volume status and diuretic dose before starting, especially if BP is low.
monitoring: Volume depletion, genital infections, ketoacidosis risk during fasting/acute illness
